meta.scope: Narrow memo on Dr. Dale William Spence Sr. and the unresolved Dovie (?) Spence parentage problem
meta.privacy_note: Avoids non-public details about living people

confidence_scale.HIGH: Directly supported by a cited public source page
confidence_scale.MEDIUM: Strong inference from multiple public sources, but no single record states the full claim

bottom_line_findings.finding: Dr. Dale William Spence Sr. is firmly established in public records as the Rice / Beaumont / Houston figure described in family context.
No source-backed public record found in this pass identifies Dale William Spence Sr.'s parents or proves that his mother was named Dovie.
Public evidence places Dale Sr. in Beaumont, Texas by 1952 at the latest.
The strongest confirmed deceased ancestor in this immediate branch remains Alice Marie Henslee Spence; Lee S. Henslee is a confirmed deceased older-generation Beaumont anchor.

public_facts.fact: Won the 1952 Texas state title in the 880 while competing for Beaumont High School
Affiliated with Rice University's Department of Human Performance and Health Sciences in 1990
Referred to by Rice News in 2019 as professor emeritus of kinesiology

notes: Inference from high-school championship year and college class years, not a direct birth record
